title: EECSL Phase H — Cross-Case Integration and Capability Lab Links

capabilityLab.label: Capability Lab Public Studio
capabilityLab.note: Use this as the public classroom simulation studio. EECSL remains the empirical case-study library.

cases.title: Higher Education IPS
cases.bestUse: Use when students need to see their own institution through public data, mission, access, cost, and structural incentives.
cases.simulation: IPS teaching simulation with SPC / University of Phoenix parameter contrast.
cases.caseQuestion: Where does a public college sit in the wider structural spectrum, and what does that reveal about mission, debt, access, and repair?

cases.title: The 2008 Financial Crisis
cases.bestUse: Use when students need to understand signal failure, rating normalization, systemic risk, and T1 vs T2 recovery.
cases.simulation: Timeline and live coherence-map simulation with T1/T2 repair controls.
cases.caseQuestion: How can a financial system preserve operational continuity while replacing the ethical function of its warning and signal channels?

cases.title: GEO Group Florida
cases.bestUse: Use when students need to examine the gap between formal legality, public safety claims, captive populations, and actual capability.
cases.simulation: Repair simulator with BioBond, Public Benefit Corporation governance, and Maathai pathway controls.
cases.caseQuestion: What would it take to move a captive-population institution from extraction architecture toward moral-commons repair?

cases.title: Florida Nursing Homes and the Visible Parasite
cases.labRoute: Care / public vulnerability / T1-T2 repair route
cases.bestUse: Use when students need to see how a care institution can measure quality while still absorbing care capacity through ownership and staffing structure.
cases.simulation: Visible Parasite repair simulator, SAGE scenario, and T1/T2 intervention classifier.
cases.caseQuestion: How can a care institution formally organized around resident protection shift risk, cost, and vulnerability onto residents, workers, families, and public systems?

chapterRoutes.title: Ethical Perception, Complexity, and the Need for Models
chapterRoutes.capabilityLab: Use the Schelling emergence simulation as the first demonstration of why ethical outcomes can exceed individual intentions.
chapterRoutes.eecsl: Preview the EECSL only lightly: explain that later chapters will use empirical simulations to examine real institutions.
chapterRoutes.classMove: Do not overload students with IPS yet. Use the contrast between simple local rules and emergent social patterns.

chapterRoutes.title: Evidence Policy, Warning, and Ethical Lenses
chapterRoutes.capabilityLab: Use the Challenger / Boisjoly classroom run mode.
chapterRoutes.eecsl: Open the 2008 Financial Crisis case for a second, larger-scale example of evidence-channel failure.
chapterRoutes.classMove: Compare engineering warning suppression with financial rating normalization.

chapterRoutes.title: Systems, Emergence, and Responsibility
chapterRoutes.capabilityLab: Use Schelling and coordination-under-heat to show layered emergence.
chapterRoutes.eecsl: Use Higher Education IPS or 2008 Financial Crisis to show how modeled parameters become empirical structural questions.
chapterRoutes.classMove: Ask how responsibility changes when harms arise from architecture rather than isolated intention.

chapterRoutes.title: Social Contract, Justice, and the Moral Commons
chapterRoutes.capabilityLab: Use the civic coordination route with Repair preset.
chapterRoutes.eecsl: Use GEO Florida as the primary empirical justice case and Higher Education IPS as a rights-to-capabilities extension.
chapterRoutes.classMove: Connect Rawls, Nussbaum, functional equivalence, John Hope Franklin, and Maathai-style shared task coordination.

chapterRoutes.title: Education, Economics, and Institutional Design
chapterRoutes.eecsl: Use Higher Education IPS as the primary empirical case.
chapterRoutes.classMove: Let students examine a public institution close enough to matter without requiring private disclosure.

chapterRoutes.title: Systems, Governance, Care, and Institutional Risk
chapterRoutes.eecsl: Use 2008 Financial Crisis and Florida Nursing Homes as paired cases.
cases: Florida Nursing Homes and the Visible Parasite
chapterRoutes.classMove: Compare operational recovery, measurement programs, and structural repair.

chapterRoutes.title: Comparative Structural Ethics
chapterRoutes.capabilityLab: Use the active analysis toolbelt: Lens Audit, Hidden Costs, Repair Builder, Navigator Bridge, Instructor Toolkit.
chapterRoutes.eecsl: Use all four empirical case modules in comparison.
cases: Florida Nursing Homes and the Visible Parasite
chapterRoutes.classMove: Ask students to distinguish data, model output, interpretation, and repair proposal across domains.

conceptThreads.question: When does agent capacity get absorbed by institutional structure?
conceptThreads.labLink: Use Capability Lab results to ask whether a simulation produced success, repair, or merely temporary coordination.

conceptThreads.question: When do signs remain operational while their ethical meaning is replaced?
conceptThreads.labLink: Use Challenger as the introductory warning-suppression example, then scale up to empirical cases.

conceptThreads.question: Does the intervention change the access condition or merely improve behavior inside the same ceiling?
conceptThreads.labLink: Use the active toolbelt Repair Builder after students run a simulation.

conceptThreads.question: Can different PRFs support the same shared task for different authentic reasons?
conceptThreads.labLink: Use Chapter 5 civic coordination with Repair preset before opening the empirical case.

conceptThreads.question: Which claims are data, computed outputs, interpretations, teaching prompts, or hypotheses?
conceptThreads.labLink: Use Capability Lab as public classroom simulation; use EECSL for evidence-governed empirical case modules.

toolbelt.purpose: Run the public classroom demonstration first.
toolbelt.output: Initial pattern, failure, or repair signature.

toolbelt.purpose: Ask what the system forms, respects, produces, distributes, exploits, and hears.
Identify who bears φ load, credibility burden, care burden, risk, delay, or loss.
Separate T1 structural repairs from T2 agent/process improvements.
Move from classroom simulation to empirical case study.
Move from public case to private student application without exposing private data.
